front: Painting: Courbet depicts himself as a Wandering Jew figure being greeted by a red-bearded patron and his servant on the road to Montpellier
back: The Meeting / Bonjour Monsieur Courbet (1854) — patron is Alfred Bruyas

front: Artist: Red-bearded Montpellier patron who helped Gustave Courbet open the 1855 Pavilion of Realism; his collection is in the Musée Fabre
Painting: Courbet's large allegorical canvas showing himself painting a landscape while his patron, Baudelaire, Proudhon, and others look on
back: The Painter's Studio (1855) — patron is Alfred Bruyas
